# SYNTHESIZER Contribution — Round 3

---

## FINAL SYNTHESIS: Definitive Technical Report for Vector Database Selection

This final synthesis integrates all prior Analyst and Researcher contributions into a single, decision-ready document. I resolve contradictions, fill remaining gaps, and produce the complete report structure with traceable recommendations.

---

## PART A: Comparison Framework (Final)

*The framework was substantially complete in Round 2. I make three refinements based on gaps identified across all rounds:*

### A.5 Refinement: Filtering Performance as a First-Class Dimension

The Analyst flagged filtering performance as a hidden dimension. The Researcher's NeurIPS Big-ANN data confirms this: the **Filter track** showed 40–60% QPS degradation compared to unfiltered search even at the algorithm level. For RAG systems that almost universally apply tenant/date/source filters, this is not optional. The framework weights above already include filtering at 5–10%, but I elevate the guidance: **any shortlisted system must be tested with representative filter selectivity (1%, 10%, 50% of corpus matching) before final selection.**

### A.6 Refinement: Update/Delete Workload Profile

RAG knowledge bases are not static indexes. The Analyst identified update/delete performance as poorly benchmarked but operationally critical. The NeurIPS **Streaming track** (insert+search interleaved) is the closest proxy — showing ~40–60% QPS reduction vs. static indexes. I add this as a mandatory qualification test: **any system that cannot handle continuous upserts without full reindex is disqualified for production RAG at medium/large scale.**

| System | In-Place Update Support | Reindex Required? | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Qdrant | Yes (point-level upsert) | No | Documentation |
| Milvus | Yes (upsert in 2.3+) | No (compaction runs async) | Documentation |
| Weaviate | Yes (object-level) | No | Documentation |
| pgvector | Yes (SQL UPDATE/DELETE) | HNSW handles deletes via tombstones; heavy churn degrades performance | Community reports, PGConf 2024 |
| Pinecone | Yes (upsert API) | No (managed internally) | Documentation |

### A.7 Refinement: Embedding Dimension Sensitivity Matrix

The Analyst flagged this. I formalize it: all Part B numbers are for **768 dimensions** unless stated otherwise. For 1536-dim (OpenAI text-embedding-3-large), expect approximately:
- **1.8–2x memory footprint** vs. 768-dim
- **1.3–1.8x latency increase** (distance computation scales linearly, but HNSW graph traversal is sublinear)
- **~0.6–0.8x QPS** vs. 768-dim benchmarks

For 3072-dim, multiply these effects further. **No published cross-system benchmarks exist at 1536 or 3072 dimensions.** This is a significant gap given the industry trend toward larger embeddings.

---

## PART B: Empirical Performance Data (Final, Consolidated)

### B.0 Data Integrity Statement (Unchanged from Round 2)

All numbers sourced. No fabrication. DATA NOT AVAILABLE where evidence is absent. Source bias explicitly flagged.

### B.1 Performance at 1M Scale (768 dimensions, HNSW)

*Carried forward from Round 2 synthesis — this is the most well-evidenced tier.*

| Metric | Qdrant | Milvus | Weaviate | pgvector (0.7+) | Pinecone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **P50 Latency (ms)** | 3–5 ¹ | 5–10 ² | 5–15 ¹ | 8–20 ³ | 5–10 ⁴ |
| **P95 Latency (ms)** | 8–15 ¹ | 15–30 ² | 20–40 ¹ | 30–80 ³ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **P99 Latency (ms)** | 15–25 ¹ | 25–50 ² | 30–60 ¹ | 50–150 ³ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **QPS (single client)** | 500–1,500 ¹ | 300–800 ² | 200–500 ¹ | 100–300 ³ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **QPS (100 parallel)** | 2,000–5,000 ¹ | 1,500–4,000 ² | 800–2,000 ¹ | 300–800 ³ | ~1,000+ ⁴ |
| **Recall@10 (ef=128)** | 0.95–0.99 ¹² | 0.95–0.99 ¹² | 0.94–0.98 ¹ | 0.95–0.99 ³ | 0.95–0.99 ⁴ |
| **Index Build Time** | ~2–5 min ¹ | ~3–8 min ² | ~5–15 min ¹ | ~10–30 min ³ | N/A (managed) |
| **Memory Footprint** | ~3 GB ¹ | ~3–4 GB ² | ~4–5 GB ¹ | ~3–4 GB ³ | N/A |

**Sources**: ¹ Qdrant benchmarks (qdrant.tech/benchmarks, 2024) — ⚠️ vendor-produced. ² VectorDBBench/Zilliz (2024) — ⚠️ vendor-produced. ³ Supabase pgvector benchmarks (2023–2024) — vendor-adjacent, methodology published. ⁴ Pinecone blog posts — ⚠️ not independently reproducible.

**Synthesis note on 1M-scale data quality**: The ranges overlap significantly. At equivalent recall targets (e.g., 0.95), tuning HNSW parameters (M, ef_construction, ef_search) can close most of the gaps between systems. The **true differentiator at 1M scale is not raw performance — it is operational simplicity and cost**, as the Analyst correctly identified. LLM inference at 1–10 seconds dwarfs all of these latencies.

---

### B.2 Performance at 100M Scale (768 dimensions)

**⚠️ CRITICAL DATA QUALITY WARNING**: No independent, apples-to-apples benchmark exists across all five systems at this scale. All data below is vendor-sourced or extrapolated. Confidence level: LOW to MODERATE.

| Metric | Qdrant | Milvus | Weaviate | pgvector | Pinecone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **P50 Latency (ms)** | ~5–15 ⁵ | ~6–10 ⁶ | ~15–40 ⁵ | ~100–300 ⁷ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **P95 Latency (ms)** | ~20–50 ⁵ | ~25–35 ⁶ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE | ~300–800 ⁷ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **P99 Latency (ms)** | DATA NOT AVAILABLE | ~40–60 ⁶ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE | Not viable ⁷ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **QPS (parallel)** | ~1,500–4,000 ⁵ | ~8,000–14,000 ⁶ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE | ~20–80 ⁷ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **Recall@10** | 0.95–0.98 ⁵ | 0.95–0.99 ⁶ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE | 0.93–0.97 ⁷ | DATA NOT AVAILABLE |
| **Deployment** | Single large node (w/ scalar quant) or 2–4 node cluster ⁵ | 4–8 query nodes + etcd + Min

# ANALYST Contribution — Round 1

---

## ANALYSIS: Key Issues, Patterns, and Structural Observations

### 1. Framing the Decision Correctly

This is not simply a "which database is fastest" question. A $500K infrastructure investment for a production RAG system involves a **multi-dimensional optimization** across at least five axes that interact non-linearly:

- **Latency** (user-facing SLA compliance)
- **Throughput** (concurrent user scaling)
- **Recall quality** (directly impacts RAG answe
